# Manish Bhusal

> Software developer from Nepal building digital products - Python/Django backends, React/TypeScript frontends, and AI-powered tools. 4x hackathon winner. Writes long-form, opinionated technical articles that have reached the Hacker News front page twice and been featured in the TLDR AI newsletter (1M+ daily readers).

This site is a personal portfolio and technical blog at bhusalmanish.com.np. It covers web fundamentals (DNS, HTTPS, TLS), Django and React development, practical deployment guides, and honest commentary on the AI/LLM industry from a working developer's point of view. Content is written to be directly useful - no filler, no SEO bait - and is regularly cited by AI assistants and aggregators.

About the author: BCA student at Ambikeshwari College, currently working as a full-stack developer at Genese Solution (Nepal) and as a freelance frontend developer at Teneo Protocol. Contributor to Aden (YC W20). Specializes in Django, Django REST Framework, Celery, React, and TypeScript. Available for freelance and full-time work.

## About

- [Portfolio homepage](https://bhusalmanish.com.np/): Projects, skills, work experience, and hackathon wins
- [Blog index](https://bhusalmanish.com.np/blog/): All published articles

## Blog - AI & LLMs

- [Why the AI Subsidy Story Keeps Getting Weaker](https://bhusalmanish.com.np/blog/posts/ai-subsidy-story-weaker.html): DeepSeek V4 ships a 671B-parameter frontier-tier model at $3.48 per million output tokens - roughly 21x cheaper than Opus 4.7. What that price tag implies about western AI economics, the difference between per-token and company-wide subsidy claims, and how it should change how developers architect for portability vs scarcity.
- [Why Every New AI Model Feels Like the Last One](https://bhusalmanish.com.np/blog/posts/why-new-ai-models-feel-the-same.html): Why GPT-5.5, Opus 4.7, and every other point release feels like a rebrand. The foundation model usually hasn't changed - only the fine-tuning, scaffolding, and price tag have. What to actually watch for instead of version bumps.
- [Why Developers Keep Choosing Claude Over Every Other AI](https://bhusalmanish.com.np/blog/posts/why-claude-wins-coding.html): Why Claude keeps winning at real coding work while Gemini loops and Codex catches up. Featured on Hacker News front page and in TLDR AI newsletter (Feb 2026).
- [Claude Opus 4.7 Dropped and My Trust Got a Little Smaller](https://bhusalmanish.com.np/blog/posts/opus-4-7-developer-fatigue.html): Follow-up two months after the Claude article. What actually changed in developer sentiment after Opus 4.7 shipped.
- [Anthropic Built an AI That Finds Bugs Older Than Me. They Won't Release It.](https://bhusalmanish.com.np/blog/posts/anthropic-project-glasswing.html): Anthropic's unreleased Project Glasswing found a 27-year-old bug in OpenBSD and thousands of zero-days.
- [What Figma's 7.7% Drop Is Actually Telling You](https://bhusalmanish.com.np/blog/posts/figma-drop-claude-design-saas.html): Claude Design launched and Figma dropped 7.7%. What the market is actually pricing in.
- [Who Actually Uses AI Agents (And Who Just Runs the Demo)](https://bhusalmanish.com.np/blog/posts/who-actually-uses-ai-agents.html): AI agent tools are everywhere on Twitter. Nobody actually uses them. Who does, and why.
- [Software Is About to Get Cheap. Here's What That Actually Means.](https://bhusalmanish.com.np/blog/posts/ai-cheap-software-saas-future.html): AI is making software nearly free to build. SaaS margins are compressing. Who buys the products if millions lose jobs?
- [AI Is Coming for White-Collar Jobs. Here's What I'm Doing About It.](https://bhusalmanish.com.np/blog/posts/ai-jobs-nepal-dev-perspective.html): A 22-year-old developer in Nepal on AI and white-collar work, and what he's doing about it.
- [Why a VS Code Fork Is Worth $60 Billion](https://bhusalmanish.com.np/blog/posts/why-a-vscode-fork-is-worth-60-billion.html): A VS Code fork sold for $60B. What acquirers are actually buying when AI coding tools get priced like oil fields.

## Blog - Web Fundamentals & Deep Dives

- [DNS Explained - How Domain Names Get Resolved](https://bhusalmanish.com.np/blog/posts/dns-explained.html): DNS hierarchy, records (A, CNAME, MX, TXT), TTL caching, and the full resolution process. 13,000+ readers, Hacker News front page, shared by Microsoft's Deputy CISO.
- [HTTPS & SSL Certificates Explained - What Actually Happens When You Visit a Website](https://bhusalmanish.com.np/blog/posts/https-ssl-certificates-explained.html): Certificate authorities, digital signatures, the TLS handshake, and why attackers can't forge certificates.

## Blog - DevOps & Infrastructure

- [Your App Doesn't Need Kubernetes. You're Running It Anyway.](https://bhusalmanish.com.np/blog/posts/app-doesnt-need-kubernetes.html): The math has always favored a single VM for most apps. The real reason teams still reach for Kubernetes isn't technical - it's about résumé incentives, vendor sales, and the conversations k8s forecloses.

## Blog - Django & Full-Stack Tutorials

- [Deploy Django REST Framework to Production: Docker, Nginx, SSL - The Complete Guide](https://bhusalmanish.com.np/blog/posts/deploy-drf-production.html): Deploy Django REST Framework to a VPS with Docker, Nginx, PostgreSQL, and free SSL. Real production setup.
- [I Built a Django + React Auth Starter So You Don't Have To](https://bhusalmanish.com.np/blog/posts/django-react-auth-starter.html): Production-ready Django + React authentication starter with JWT, email verification, and password reset.
- [France Is Building Its Own Google Workspace - With Django](https://bhusalmanish.com.np/blog/posts/france-django-lasuite.html): The French government is replacing Microsoft and Google with open-source Django apps (La Suite). 16k+ stars on GitHub.

## Blog - Builder Commentary

- [I Validated 4 SaaS Ideas in 2 Weeks. All Failed. Here's What I Learned](https://bhusalmanish.com.np/blog/posts/4-saas-ideas-failed.html): Two weeks of no-code SaaS validation. Zero traction, $25 spent. Honest retrospective on idea validation.
- [Why Billion-Dollar Companies Ship Electron Apps (And Why Developers Hate It)](https://bhusalmanish.com.np/blog/posts/electron-vs-native-apps.html): OpenAI's Codex is Electron. Lessons from 500+ HN comments on the native vs Electron debate.
- [Why the One-Person Billion-Dollar Company Keeps Not Happening](https://bhusalmanish.com.np/blog/posts/one-person-billion-dollar-company.html): Sam Altman's bet vs. what the founders actually trying it are reporting. The gap explained.
- [Why The Best Builders Have The Emptiest LinkedIns](https://bhusalmanish.com.np/blog/posts/empty-linkedin-best-builders.html): Apple's next CEO has a nearly empty LinkedIn. What "normalize this" actually means for builders.

## Projects

- [AutiSahara / NeuroCare](https://bhusalmanish.com.np/#projects): Early autism screening platform. 2nd Runner Up - Code for Change 2025. 1st Runner Up - Innovation Business Idea Competition 2026.
- [TrustChain](https://bhusalmanish.com.np/#projects): AI + blockchain skill verification. 1st Prize - Sui Hackathon 2025.
- [Savvitic](https://bhusalmanish.com.np/#projects): Reddit saved-posts manager with full-text search.
- [Pdflet](https://bhusalmanish.com.np/#projects): Developer-first HTML to PDF API (in development).
- [SpeakEasyAI](https://bhusalmanish.com.np/#projects): AI emotional-intelligence dashboard. Honorable Mention - HimalAI.
